column1: # Cross-Framework Mapping EU AI Act (Regulation (EU) 2024/1689) <-> ISO/IEC 27001:2022
# Source: EU AI Act — published 13 June 2024 in OJEU
column2: in force 1 August 2024. Phased application through 2 August 2027.

column1: # Crosswalk curated by Senior-GRC-Consultant (rewritten 2026-04-17 for loader-native IDs).
# Quality note: AI-Act obligations extend well beyond the ISMS remit — pair with ISO/IEC 42001:2023 (AI MS).

column9: AI risk classification — ISO Clause 6.1.2 risk-assessment process provides the generic risk discipline.
High-risk AI requirements — Clause 6.1.3 risk treatment selects controls; AI Act adds fundamental-rights controls.
Data governance — A.5.12 classification; AI Act imposes bias/representativeness analyses beyond ISO.
Information access restriction supports data governance.
Technical documentation — Clause 7.5 documented information; Annex IV adds AI-specific content.
Transparency obligations — A.5.1 policy discipline for user-facing info.
Human oversight — A.5.2 roles and responsibilities; AI Act requires independent review.
Accuracy, robustness, cybersecurity — A.8.29 security testing; AI-specific adversarial robustness extra.
Cybersecurity leg of Art.15 — A.8.8 vulnerability management.
Conformity assessment — Clause 9.2 internal audit supports conformity evidence.
Post-market monitoring — A.8.16 monitoring activities as baseline.
GPAI model obligations — A.5.20 supplier agreements cover distribution contracts.
Serious incident reporting — A.5.24 incident management.
Threat intelligence for AI risk classification — A.5.7.
